name: color-token-manager
description: Extract hardcoded colors into tokens, manage colors.ts, preview refactors, WCAG contrast checks, and export design tokens for React Native and TypeScript.

colorTokenManager.enabledLanguages.description: Languages where Color Token Manager should scan for hardcoded colors.

colorTokenManager.languageMode.description: Controls how aggressively Color Token Manager scans and replaces colors across languages.

colorTokenManager.colorsFilePath.description: Legacy custom path to colors.ts, relative to the workspace folder of the active file. Use tokenFilePath for theme.ts, tokens.ts, or design token files.

colorTokenManager.colorsFile.description: Preferred path to the colors file, relative to the workspace folder of the active file. Takes priority over colorsFilePath and other legacy token file settings.

colorTokenManager.tokenFilePath.description: Custom path to the token/theme file, relative to the workspace folder of the active file. Supports colors.ts, theme.ts, themes.ts, tokens.ts, and designTokens.ts.

colorTokenManager.themeFilePath.description: Preferred path to the theme file, relative to the workspace folder of the active file. Use this when theme tokens live in a different file from colors.

colorTokenManager.tokenFile.description: Path to the color or theme token file (e.g., src/theme/theme.ts, src/tokens/colors.ts). If empty, Color Token Manager will try to detect it automatically. Takes priority over tokenFilePath and colorsFilePath.

colorTokenManager.themeFile.description: Preferred path to the theme token file (e.g., src/theme/theme.ts). Takes priority over themeFilePath and tokenFilePath when set.

colorTokenManager.projectWorkflow.description: Remembers whether the current workspace is using colors only, theme only, or separate colors plus theme files.

colorTokenManager.tokenExportName.description: Token object export to read from the selected token file. Use auto, colors, theme, themes, tokens, designTokens, or a custom variable name.

colorTokenManager.tokenObject.description: Exported object name to read/write tokens from (e.g., colors, theme, tokens, lightTheme). Takes priority over tokenExportName. Used as the named import identifier.

colorTokenManager.tokenFileKind.description: Structural style of the token file. auto detects from the file contents. colors suits flat/nested color objects. theme suits light/dark theme objects. tokens suits generic design-token files.

colorTokenManager.importPath.description: Custom import path for colors. If empty, calculate relative import path automatically.

colorTokenManager.generatedNamePrefix.description: Optional prefix for generated token names.

colorTokenManager.namingStrategy.description: Controls generated token names. semantic can generate nested paths like button.background; contextValue includes context and color value; valueOnly uses only the color value.

colorTokenManager.colorSeriesMode.description: Controls whether extracted colors are named as flat tokens (off), semantic theme tokens (semantic), numeric color scales like primary.500 (scale), or detected automatically (auto).

colorTokenManager.tokenPathMode.description: Controls whether generated token references are flat or nested. auto matches the existing colors.ts shape so flat projects do not receive colors.text.black references.

colorTokenManager.autoReplaceExistingCol.description: When true, replace hardcoded colors with existing matching tokens automatically.

colorTokenManager.excludeGlobs.description: Additional glob patterns to skip during folder preview and extraction.

colorTokenManager.createSemanticAliases.description: When true, create semantic alias tokens like buttonBackground: colors.primary for existing color values used in a new context.

colorTokenManager.createBackupsBeforeFol.description: When true, write timestamped backups before folder-wide extraction applies changes.

colorTokenManager.enableInlineSwatches.description: When true, show inline color swatches next to hardcoded colors in supported source files.

colorTokenManager.enableContrastDiagnost.description: When true, warn in colors.ts when text-like tokens do not meet the configured WCAG contrast target against background tokens.

colorTokenManager.contrastLevel.description: WCAG contrast level used by color token diagnostics. AA requires 4.5:1 and AAA requires 7:1 for normal text.

colorTokenManager.contrastBackgroundToke.description: Optional token keys to use as contrast backgrounds, such as background.white. If empty, background-like tokens are detected automatically.

colorTokenManager.tokenLayerMode.description: When semanticFirst, new extracted colors create primitive tokens and semantic aliases, such as primitive.gray.gray500 and semantic.text.muted.

colorTokenManager.themeTokenPrefix.description: Optional theme prefix for generated semantic token paths. Example: dark creates colors.dark.background.primary in flat mode or colors.semantic.dark.background.primary in semantic-first mode.

colorTokenManager.importStyle.deprecationMessage: Use colorTokenManager.importMode instead. This setting will be removed in v1.0.0.

colorTokenManager.importMode.description: Controls generated import syntax for colors. Use named for `import { colors }`, default for default imports, and namespace for `import * as colors`.

colorTokenManager.importIdentifier.description: Identifier used when inserting color references and imports. This is the visible symbol used in generated code.

colorTokenManager.referencePrefix.description: Prefix used when generating replacement references in code, such as `colors`, `theme.colors`, or `tokens.color`. Defaults to tokenObject or importIdentifier.

colorTokenManager.extractEmbeddedColors.description: When true, also extract colors embedded inside longer quoted CSS-like strings, such as `boxShadow: '0 0 4px #000'`.

scripts.compile: npm run check && npm run bundle
scripts.test: npm run test:compile && npm run test:run
scripts.test:run: node --require ./test/setup.cjs --test out-test/test/smoke.test.js out-test/test/colorFile.test.js out-test/test/colorExtractor.test.js out-test/test/globUtils.test.js out-test/test/workspaceUtils.test.js out-test/test/projectRouting.test.js out-test/test/setup.test.js out-test/test/dashboard.test.js out-test/test/mcpServer.test.js out-test/test/themeAudit.test.js out-test/test/diagnostics.test.js out-test/test/themeTokenSupport.test.js out-test/test/tokenDetection.test.js out-test/test/tokenNaming.test.js out-test/test/importUtils.test.js out-test/test/cssAdapter.test.js out-test/test/htmlAdapter.test.js out-test/test/javascriptTypescriptAdapters.test.js out-test/test/previewOnlyAdapters.test.js out-test/test/languages/registry.test.js out-test/test/languages/settings.test.js
scripts.test:integration: npm run compile && npm run test:integration:compile && node ./out-test/test/runTest.js
